title.en: GST Registration Basics — Thresholds, Documents, and Whether You Even Need It

answerBox.en: GST registration is mandatory once turnover crosses ₹40 lakh for goods (₹20 lakh for services; ₹10–20 lakh in special-category states) — and immediately, regardless of turnover, for interstate goods supply and selling through e-commerce operators. Registration is free on gst.gov.in and takes about a week with Aadhaar authentication. Below thresholds, weigh voluntary registration: input credit and B2B credibility vs monthly compliance.

en.title: Do you actually need it?
en.summary: Thresholds, compulsory categories, and the freelancer nuances.
en.detail: Mandatory when: aggregate turnover crosses **₹40 lakh (goods)** / **₹20 lakh (services)** (₹20L/₹10L in special-category states); OR regardless of turnover for **interstate supply of goods**, **selling via e-commerce operators** (Amazon/Flipkart sellers), casual/non-resident taxable persons, and reverse-charge-liable recipients. Key service nuance: **interstate services** stay exempt up to ₹20 lakh — freelancers serving clients across India under that figure need not register. **Exports are interstate supplies** — but service exporters under ₹20 lakh remain exempt from registration.

en.title: Register: free, online, Aadhaar-fast
en.summary: PAN, Aadhaar, bank, address proof — REG-01 on the portal.
en.detail: On **[gst.gov.in](https://www.gst.gov.in)**: Part A (PAN + mobile/email OTP), Part B (REG-01) with the business details, **principal place of business proof** (electricity bill + rent agreement/NOC for rented premises — our ₹199 NOC/consent drafting helps here), bank details, photos, and authorised-signatory data. **Aadhaar-authenticate** for the ~7-working-day track (skipping it invites physical verification and ~30 days). GSTIN + certificate download on approval. The government charges **nothing** — "GST registration fees" quoted online are agent fees.

en.title: Choose the scheme: regular vs composition
en.summary: Input credit + B2B vs flat low tax and light filing.
en.detail: **Regular**: charge GST at your rate, claim **input tax credit**, file GSTR-1/3B (monthly, or **QRMP** quarterly-with-monthly-payment below ₹5 crore). **Composition** (goods to ₹1.5 crore; services to ₹50 lakh): flat 1% (traders/manufacturers), 5% (restaurants), 6% (services) on turnover, minimal filing (CMP-08 quarterly) — but **no input credit, no interstate supply, no tax collection** from customers. B2B businesses and exporters live on the regular scheme; small local B2C often fits composition.

en.title: After the GSTIN: the hygiene that avoids notices
en.summary: Invoices, returns even if nil, e-way bills — and cancellation.
en.detail: Compliance floor: GST-compliant **invoices** (GSTIN, HSN/SAC, rate-wise breakup), returns **even when nil** (non-filing auto-cancels registrations and blocks e-way bills), reconcile input credit against **GSTR-2B** monthly, and generate **e-way bills** for goods movement ₹50,000+. Display the certificate at premises and GSTIN on signage/website. Winding down or dipping below thresholds: apply for **cancellation** properly and file the final return — abandoned GSTINs breed notices for years. A ₹299 consultation maps your first-year calendar.

rows.label: Registration on gst.gov.in
rows.note: Agent "fees" are for their service, not govt

en.footnote: This is a legal-process overview, not tax advice — rates, thresholds and scheme fit change with notifications and your specific supplies; confirm current positions with a GST practitioner before acting.

en.q: I freelance for clients in other states, earning ₹12 lakh. Must I register?
en.a: No — interstate SERVICES enjoy the ₹20 lakh threshold exemption (unlike interstate goods, which trigger registration from rupee one). Track aggregate turnover across all your income sources though; crossing ₹20 lakh obliges registration within 30 days.

en.q: Do I need GST to sell on Amazon/Flipkart?
en.a: For goods, yes — supplying through an e-commerce operator requires registration regardless of turnover (the platforms enforce it at onboarding). Limited relaxations exist for certain intra-state service suppliers via ECOs. Factor the monthly compliance before listing.

en.q: Should I register voluntarily below the threshold?
en.a: Register if: your clients are B2B (they want your GSTIN and credit), you claim input credit on significant purchases, or tenders/platforms demand it. Stay out if you serve B2C locally under threshold — voluntary registration binds you to full compliance with no exit until cancellation.

en.q: What happens if I crossed the threshold months ago and never registered?
en.a: Liability runs from the day you became liable: expect registration with effect from then, tax on the intervening supplies (possibly from your pocket — you didn't collect it), interest, and penalty (10% of tax or ₹10,000 minimum; higher for suppression). Regularise voluntarily before a notice finds you — the economics only worsen.

en.q: Rented office and the landlord won't give an electricity bill. What works as address proof?
en.a: The standard stack: rent agreement + landlord's NOC/consent letter + any utility bill/property-tax receipt of the premises (in the landlord's name is fine). Our ₹199 drafting covers the NOC and consent formats registrars accept; co-working spaces issue their own standard letters.

reviewer: Adv. Arjun Nair, Bar Council of Kerala, KER/0973/2012
author: WakilBhai Editorial Team
